title: taraxacum iberanthum
intro: Taraxacum iberanthum, also known as the Iberian dandelion, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of Western Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high content of taraxasterol and other sesquiterpenes. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure, improving liver function, and supporting digestive health. Taraxacum iberanthum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots can also be used in herbal remedies to support overall well-being.
